prs.title: [NVFP4][Hopper/AMD Instinct] Add Triton kernels for NVFP4 dequantization and QDQ emulation
prs.author: fxmarty-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Add Triton kernels for NVFP4 dequantization and quantize-dequantize emulation to accelerate NVFP4 quantization emulation on devices that do not support this dtype natively (AMD Instinct MI3xx, H100), following https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35733 and https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35737.

The Triton kernels automatically replace the torch eager reference implementation when running on CUDA devices, with significant perf improvements, and avoids launching many independent kernel when using `--enforce-eager`, with back and forth HBM read/write.

These fused kernels  also help to stop hitting https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/180341 and https://github.com/ROCm/rocm-systems/issues/5071 where many stacked kernels calls from `dequantize_to_dtype` and `ref_nvfp4_quant_dequant` result in HSA crashes in recent ROCm versions / pytorch nightly (`vllm bench serve` with NVFP4 emulation on MI355X is not runnable without `HSA_TOOLS_DISABLE_REGISTER=1` due to these crashes - adding this env var is very slow).


This PR is submitted independently from https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35737, but closely related to it.

## Test Plan

```python
pytest tests/models/quantization/test_nvfp4.py::test_triton_dequantize_nvfp4 -vvvvv -s 
pytest tests/models/quantization/test_nvfp4.py::test_triton_nvfp4_quant_dequant -vvvvv -s
```

## Test Result

The tests verify:
1. Correctness: Triton kernels produce bitwise-identical results to CPU reference (atol=0, rtol=0)
2. Coverage: Tests multiple tensor shapes (2D/3D), sizes, and global scale values
3. Real weights: Dequantization test uses actual NVFP4 weights from `nvidia/Qwen3-30B-A3B-NVFP4` checkpoint
4. Performance: Benchmarks show speedup vs. CPU reference implementation

## `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=7 pytest tests/models/quantization/test_nvfp4.py -s -vvvvv -k "test_triton_dequantize_nvfp4"`

```
  dequantize 2D base [512, 1024]:
    triton:    median=0.007ms, min=0.0

prs.title: [LoRA] MoE LoRA Refactor
prs.author: jeejeelee
prs.body_head:
## Motivation


Currently, MoE LoRA is wired in by monkey-patching methods on the modular kernel at construction time. `FusedMoEWithLoRA._inject_lora_into_fused_moe` wraps `FusedMoEKernel.apply`, `TritonExperts.activation`, and `TritonExperts.moe_sum` with `fwd_decorator` / `act_decorator` / `moe_sum_decorator`, and smuggles tensors between them through  `moe_state_dict`. This has several concrete problems:

### 1. Hacky and hard to maintain.
The LoRA contribution is hidden inside decorators on base-MoE methods. Someone reading the MoE experts code sees `self.activation(...)` and `self.moe_sum(...)` — they have no syntactic hint that these calls may actually run LoRA shrink/expand GEMMs, or that  `moe_state_dict` carries cross-call state between them. Debugging requires holding the patching order in your head.

### 2. MoE changes don't see LoRA
 Because the LoRA path lives outside the expert `apply()` functions, any refactor of `TritonExperts` / `UnfusedOAITritonExperts` / `MarlinExperts` has to re-discover the LoRA contract (what `activation` receives, what `moe_sum` receives, what shape `intermediate_cache*` has). Every MoE-side change risks breaking LoRA  silently.

### 3. Hard to extend
  Adding support for new features — EP, additional quantized backends, new expert impls — means replicating or working around the decorator chain, and the state-dict plumbing assumes one specific control flow. There is no extension point for an expert that wants to apply LoRA at a different point .

## Change


Treat LoRA as a `first-class` concern in the modular MoE kernel rather than an external patch.

### 1. `MoELoRAContext`: a single explicit payload

A new `MoELoRAContext` dataclass (`vllm/lora/lora_context.py`) packages all of
the LoRA state a MoE forward pass needs:

- `w13_lora_a_stacked` / `w13_lora_b_stacked` / `w2_lora_a_stacked` /
  `w2_lora_b_stacked`
- `adapter_enabled`, `max_loras`
- routing/sharding info: `top_k`, `w13_num_slices`

prs.title: [CI][AMD]BugFix] Fix deadlock occuring in test_moe_layer
prs.author: rasmith
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
`test_moe_layer` is hitting a deadlock on MI300 during execution of the` test_moe_layer::test_moe_layer` tests which spawn multiple workers for these tests which use `TP=2,4`. There is a `WeakValueDictionary` [here](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/distributed/device_communicators/base_device_communicator.py#L15) which caches `DeepEP` buffers [here](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/distributed/device_communicators/all2all.py#L245) and when garbage collection runs, these weak references can be non-deterministically collected.  The `DeepEP` buffer's that were collected will result in a no-op when they are [called](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/distributed/device_communicators/base_device_communicator.py#L25), while those that were not collected will wait indefinitely.  This results in the parallel workers with buffers that weren't collected deadlocking, since they are unable to make progress, and eventually the following error message will be seen:

`DeepEP error: CPU recv timeout`

This PR disables garbage collection for this test to avoid this problem during testing which fixes the deadlock issue.
## Test Plan
` pytest -sv kernels/moe/test_moe_layer.py::test_moe_layer`
## Test Result
No deadlocks result.

` 12 passed, 20 skipped, 17 warnings in 349.84s`


---

prs.title: [ROCm] Fuse Q/K RMSNorm in MLA attention via AITER `fused_qk_rmsnorm`
prs.author: xaguilar-amd
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Fuse the two separate RMSNorm kernel launches (`q_a_layernorm` and `kv_a_layernorm`) in Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) into a single AITER `fused_qk_rmsnorm` kernel on ROCm. This eliminates one GPU kernel launch per transformer layer per decode step for DeepSeek V2/V3/Kimi models.

## Motivation

During decode on MI3XX with DeepSeek V3 / Kimi-K2.5-MXFP4, the MLA attention block performs two consecutive RMSNorm operations on the compressed latent tensors:

1. `q_a_layernorm(q_c)` -- normalizes the compressed query latent (dim=512)
2. `kv_a_layernorm(kv_c)` -- normalizes the compressed key-value latent (dim=1536)

Each is a separate GPU kernel launch. At decode time, where per-kernel compute is ~4-5us but kernel launch overhead is significant, eliminating launches directly improves latency.

AITER provides a `fused_qk_rmsnorm` kernel that fuses both operations into a single launch using a 2D grid where `blockIdx.y` selects the Q (y=0) or K (y=1) normalization path, running both in parallel across different CUs.

**Impact**: Eliminates 1 kernel launch per layer per step. With 61 layers in DeepSeek V3, this saves 61 kernel launches per decode step.


## Design decisions

- **Model-level change, not a fusion pass**: The existing `QKNormRoPEFusionPass` targets a different pattern (standard QKV per-head norm + RoPE). The MLA pattern has different tensor shapes (latent-space norms on dims 512 and 1536, not per-head norms) and the two norms are separated from RoPE by a GEMM (`q_b_proj`). A direct model-level change in `mla.py` is more reliable than a graph pattern match.
- **Guarded behind AITER availability**: The fused path only activates when all conditions are met: ROCm platform, AITER installed, AITER rmsnorm enabled via env var. All other platforms and configurations use the existing two-norm path with zero behavioral change.
- **AITER's built-in fallback**: The AITER `fused_qk_rmsnorm` function automatically falls back to two separ

prs.title: [Aiter][ROCm] gdn_linear_attn kernel fusion
prs.author: tpopp
prs.body_head:
With more recent aiter kernels, this combines more kernels together, reducing kernel overhead and optimizing further the computations.

For Qwen3Next, the lm_eval accuracy remains consistent at 0.86 and 0.82 for flexible/strict matching and performance improves by 1-4% depending on batch size.

prs.title: [Quantization] quantized kv cache loading for models like qwen
prs.author: xuebwang-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose This PR aims to fix quantized kv cache model loading issues using AMD-Quark. The fix was initially applied to the `gpt-oss` model and may be extended to other models in `vllm/model_executor/models`, as they share a nearly identical methodology. Here is the original error traceback w/o this PR fixing. ## Here is a check list of some models defined in `vllm/model_executor/models/` for the supportness of quantized kv cache loading. Models with `get_cache_scale` called is marked as ✅, while models without `get_cache_scale` called is marked as ⚠️. The ⚠️ symbol is preferred over ❌ since some models can intrinsically have non-quantized KV cache, for instance: - VL models - MLA models - _etc_ Partial list is: ⚠️baichuan ⚠️bloom ✅ dbrx ⚠️ deepseek ⚠️falcon ⚠️ gemma ✅ gemma2 ✅ gemma3 ✅ gpt_j ⚠️ gpt_neox ⚠️ **_gpt_oss_**: this PR initially aims for ✅ grok1 ✅ hunyuan_v1 ✅ llama ✅ llama4 ✅ mixtral ⚠️ mixtral ✅ phimoe ⚠️ qwen ✅ qwen2 ⚠️ qwen2_moe ⚠️ qwen3_moe ... ## Test Plan ## Test Result ---

prs.title: [ROCm][fusion] enable ROCm rms_norm pattern matching in qk_norm_rope fusion
prs.author: gbyu-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Current qk_norm_rope fusion can only match the vLLM custom cuda rms_norm. This pr enables AITER custom rms_norm pattern matching for ROCm backends. 

## Test Plan
```bash
#!/bin/bash

export SAFETENSORS_FAST_GPU=1
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1
export VLLM_RPC_TIMEOUT=1800000
export VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL=DEBUG

vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B \
    --tensor-parallel-size 1 \
    --max-num-batched-tokens 32768 \
    --trust-remote-code \
    --no-enable-prefix-caching \
    --gpu_memory_utilization 0.9 \
    --compilation-config '{"cudagraph_mode": "FULL_AND_PIECEWISE", "pass_config": {"enable_qk_norm_rope_fusion": 1, "enable_noop": true}, "custom_ops": ["+rms_norm", "+rotary_embedding"]}' \
    --async-scheduling
```

## Test Result
Below log can be observed from the server side:
```bash
(EngineCore_DP0 pid=349086)^[[0;0m DEBUG 11-23 13:46:08 [compilation/qk_norm_rope_fusion.py:235] Fused QK Norm+RoPE on 1 sites
```

Dataset acc with main:
```bash
|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|     5|exact_match|↑  |0.4185|±  |0.0136|
|     |       |strict-match    |     5|exact_match|↑  |0.4193|±  |0.0136|
```

Dataset acc with this pr:
```bash
|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|     5|exact_match|↑  |0.4139|±  |0.0136|
|     |       |strict-match    |     5|exact_match|↑  |0.4139|±  |0.0136|
```
